title: Spicy Silky Syrian Red Pepper Stir-fry
description: A vibrant, low-sodium Syrian-inspired stir-fry that balances the heat of red peppers with a silky sauce, perfect for a quick, comforting meal.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium‑high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger; sauté until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add sliced red onion and cook 2–3 minutes until translucent.
Toss in sliced red bell pepper and stir‑fry 3–4 minutes until tender but still crisp.
Whisk together low‑sodium soy sauce, tomato paste, honey, lemon juice, smoked paprika, ground cumin, and crushed red pepper flakes until smooth.
Pour the sauce over the vegetables, stir to coat, and cook another 2–3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Taste and adjust seasoning if desired; keep it low‑sodium by avoiding additional salt.
Remove from heat and serve immediately over rice or flatbread.
